[[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:kk]][[en:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:redirect]] [[en:Sasazuka Kofun]][[en:Sasazuka Kofun]][[ja:&action=edit]] [[Category:new pages not of uncertain originality]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:qqq]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:qqq]]

{{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sasazuka Kofun | Group = Higashiya Kofun Cluster | image = [[File:Sasazuka Kofun.JPG|250px]] | image Caption = Sasazuka Kofun | location = Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|28|37|N|139|54|7|E}} | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 100 m, Height: 10.5 m | Construction Era = Mid-5th century | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}} | Designated Cultural Property = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Sasazuka Kofun) }}
'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's shape, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
Artifacts recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other burial mounds, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively forming the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. Artifacts include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and shape are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it forms the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Tochigi Prefecture-designated cultural properties|ja|栃木県指定文化財一覧}} * {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} * {{ill|Tsukayama Kofun Cluster|ja|塚山古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}} * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "朝顔形埴輪}}" to "朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}"
revision.text:
{{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sasazuka Kofun | Group = Higashiya Kofun Cluster | image = [[File:Sasazuka Kofun.JPG|250px]] | image Caption = Sasazuka Kofun | location = Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|28|37|N|139|54|7|E}} | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 100 m, Height: 10.5 m | Construction Era = Mid-5th century | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}}, {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}} | Designated Cultural Property = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Sasazuka Kofun) }}
'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's shape, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
Artifacts recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other burial mounds, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively forming the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. Artifacts include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and shape are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it forms the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Tochigi Prefecture-designated cultural properties|ja|栃木県指定文化財一覧}} * {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} * {{ill|Tsukayama Kofun Cluster|ja|塚山古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}} * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}

[[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:kk]][[en:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:redirect]] [[en:Sasazuka Kofun]][[en:Sasazuka Kofun]][[ja:&action=edit]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:new pages]] [[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:qqq]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:qqq]]

{{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sasazuka Kofun | Group = Higashiya Kofun Cluster | image = [[File:Sasazuka Kofun.JPG|250px]] | image Caption = Sasazuka Kofun | location = Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|28|37|N|139|54|7|E}} | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 100 m, Height: 10.5 m | Construction Era = Mid-5th century | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}} | Designated Cultural Property = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Sasazuka Kofun) }}
'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's shape, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
Artifacts recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other burial mounds, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively forming the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. Artifacts include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and shape are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it forms the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Tochigi Prefecture-designated cultural properties|ja|栃木県指定文化財一覧}} * {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} * {{ill|Tsukayama Kofun Cluster|ja|塚山古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}} * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}

=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and shape are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it forms the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

[[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:100 oldest pages]][[Category:kk]][[en:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:redirect]] [[en:Sasazuka Kofun]][[en:Sasazuka Kofun]][[ja:&action=edit]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:new pages]] [[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:qqq]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun]][[Category:qqq]]

{{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sasazuka Kofun | Group = Higashiya Kofun Cluster | image = [[File:Sasazuka Kofun.JPG|250px]] | image Caption = Sasazuka Kofun | location = Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|28|37|N|139|54|7|E}} | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 100 m, Height: 10.5 m | Construction Era = Mid-5th century | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}} | Designated Cultural Property = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Sasazuka Kofun) }}
'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's shape, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
Artifacts recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other burial mounds, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively forming the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. Artifacts include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and shape are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it forms the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Tochigi Prefecture-designated cultural properties|ja|栃木県指定文化財一覧}} * {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} * {{ill|Tsukayama Kofun Cluster|ja|塚山古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}} * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}

Nearby are several other burial mounds, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively forming the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. Artifacts include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and shape are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it forms the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

{{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sasazuka Kofun | Group = Higashiya Kofun Cluster | image = [[File:Sasazuka Kofun.JPG|250px]] | image Caption = Sasazuka Kofun | location = Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|28|37|N|139|54|7|E}} | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 100 m, Height: 10.5 m | Construction Era = Mid-5th century | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}} | Designated Cultural Property = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Sasazuka Kofun) }}
'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's shape, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
Artifacts recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other burial mounds, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively forming the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. Artifacts include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and shape are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it forms the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.

The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's shape, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
Artifacts recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other burial mounds, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively forming the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. Artifacts include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and shape are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it forms the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Tochigi Prefecture-designated cultural properties|ja|栃木県指定文化財一覧}} * {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} * {{ill|Tsukayama Kofun Cluster|ja|塚山古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}} * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}

{{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sasazuka Kofun | Group = Higashiya Kofun Cluster | image = [[File:Sasazuka Kofun.JPG|250px]] | image Caption = Sasazuka Kofun | location = Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|28|37|N|139|54|7|E}} | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 100 m, Height: 10.5 m | Construction Era = Mid-5th century | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}} | Designated Cultural Property = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Sasazuka Kofun) }}
'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's shape, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
Artifacts recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other burial mounds, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively forming the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. Artifacts include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and shape are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it forms the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Tochigi Prefecture-designated cultural properties|ja|栃木県指定文化財一覧}} * {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} * {{ill|Tsukayama Kofun Cluster|ja|塚山古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}} * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}

Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's shape, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
Artifacts recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other burial mounds, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively forming the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. Artifacts include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and shape are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it forms the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

{{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sasazuka Kofun | Group = Higashiya Kofun Cluster | image = [[File:Sasazuka Kofun.JPG|250px]] | image Caption = Sasazuka Kofun | location = Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|28|37|N|139|54|7|E}} | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 100 m, Height: 10.5 m | Construction Era = Mid-5th century | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}} | Designated Cultural Property = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Sasazuka Kofun) }}
'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's shape, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
Artifacts recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other burial mounds, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively forming the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. Artifacts include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and shape are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it forms the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. Artifacts include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and shape are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it forms the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Tochigi Prefecture-designated cultural properties|ja|栃木県指定文化財一覧}} * {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} * {{ill|Tsukayama Kofun Cluster|ja|塚山古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}} * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}

{{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sasazuka Kofun | Group = Higashiya Kofun Cluster | image = [[File:Sasazuka Kofun.JPG|250px]] | image Caption = Sasazuka Kofun | location = Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|28|37|N|139|54|7|E}} | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 100 m, Height: 10.5 m | Construction Era = Mid-5th century | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}} | Designated Cultural Property = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Sasazuka Kofun) }}
'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's shape, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
Artifacts recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other burial mounds, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively forming the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. Artifacts include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and shape are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it forms the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Tochigi Prefecture-designated cultural properties|ja|栃木県指定文化財一覧}} * {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} * {{ill|Tsukayama Kofun Cluster|ja|塚山古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}} * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}

'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

{{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sasazuka Kofun | Group = Higashiya Kofun Cluster | image = [[File:Sasazuka Kofun.JPG|250px]] | image Caption = Sasazuka Kofun | location = Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|28|37|N|139|54|7|E}} | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 100 m, Height: 10.5 m | Construction Era = Mid-5th century | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}} | Designated Cultural Property = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Sasazuka Kofun) }}
'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's shape, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
Artifacts recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other burial mounds, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively forming the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. Artifacts include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and shape are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it forms the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

{{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sasazuka Kofun | Group = Higashiya Kofun Cluster | image = [[File:Sasazuka Kofun.JPG|250px]] | image Caption = Sasazuka Kofun | location = Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|28|37|N|139|54|7|E}} | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 100 m, Height: 10.5 m | Construction Era = Mid-5th century | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}} | Designated Cultural Property = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Sasazuka Kofun) }}
'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's shape, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
Artifacts recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other burial mounds, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively forming the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. Artifacts include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and shape are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it forms the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Tochigi Prefecture-designated cultural properties|ja|栃木県指定文化財一覧}} * {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} * {{ill|Tsukayama Kofun Cluster|ja|塚山古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}} * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}

{{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sasazuka Kofun | Group = Higashiya Kofun Cluster | image = [[File:Sasazuka Kofun.JPG|250px]] | image Caption = Sasazuka Kofun | location = Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|28|37|N|139|54|7|E}} | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 100 m, Height: 10.5 m | Construction Era = Mid-5th century | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}} | Designated Cultural Property = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Sasazuka Kofun) }}
'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's shape, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
Artifacts recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively forming the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. Artifacts include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and shape are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it forms the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Tochigi Prefecture-designated cultural properties|ja|栃木県指定文化財一覧}} * {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} * {{ill|Tsukayama Kofun Cluster|ja|塚山古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}} * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}
