'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-typed kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-typed kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-typed moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
Artifacts recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} * {{ill|Morning glory-typed haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-typed haniwa}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively forming the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. Artifacts include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-typed kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-typed keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it forms the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sasazuka Kofun | part_of = Higashiya Kofun Cluster | image = [[File:Sasazuka Kofun.JPG|250px]] | image Caption = Sasazuka Kofun | location = Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|36|28|37|N|139|54|7|E}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 100 m, Height: 10.5 m | built = Mid-5th century | Buried Figure = | Artifacts = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}} | Designated Cultural Property = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Sasazuka Kofun) }}
'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
Artifacts recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively forming the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. Artifacts include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it forms the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Tochigi Prefecture-designated cultural properties|ja|栃木県指定文化財一覧}} * {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} * {{ill|Tsukayama Kofun Cluster|ja|塚山古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}} * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}

'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
Artifacts recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was designated a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively forming the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. Artifacts include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it forms the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Tochigi Prefecture-designated cultural properties|ja|栃木県指定文化財一覧}} * {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} * {{ill|Tsukayama Kofun Cluster|ja|塚山古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}} * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}

'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
Artifacts recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was designated a Tochigi Prefecture other_designation.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively forming the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. Artifacts include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it forms the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Tochigi Prefecture-designated cultural properties|ja|栃木県指定文化財一覧}} * {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} * {{ill|Tsukayama Kofun Cluster|ja|塚山古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}} * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}

This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.

=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it forms the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
Artifacts recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was designated a Tochigi Prefecture other_designation.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively forming the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. Artifacts include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it forms the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
Artifacts recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was designated a Tochigi Prefecture other_designation.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively forming the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. Artifacts include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it forms the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was designated a Tochigi Prefecture other_designation.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively forming the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it forms the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was designated a Tochigi Prefecture other_designation.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively forming the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it forms the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sasazuka Kofun | part_of = Higashiya Kofun Cluster | image = [[File:Sasazuka Kofun.JPG|250px]] | caption = Sasazuka Kofun | location = Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|36|28|37|N|139|54|7|E}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 100 m, Height: 10.5 m | built = Mid-5th century | Buried Figure = | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}} | Designated Cultural Property = Tochigi Prefecture Designated other_designation (Sasazuka Kofun) }}
'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was designation1 a Tochigi Prefecture other_designation.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively forming the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it forms the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Tochigi Prefecture-designation1 cultural properties|ja|栃木県指定文化財一覧}} * {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} * {{ill|Tsukayama Kofun Cluster|ja|塚山古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}} * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}

'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was other_designation1 a Tochigi Prefecture other_other_designation.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was other_designation1 a Tochigi Prefecture other_other_designation.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

revision.text:

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sasazuka Kofun | part_of = Higashiya Kofun Cluster | image = [[File:Sasazuka Kofun.JPG|250px]] | caption = Sasazuka Kofun | location = Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|36|28|37|N|139|54|7|E}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 100 m, Height: 10.5 m | built = Mid-5th century | Buried Figure = | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}} | Designated Cultural Property = Tochigi Prefecture Designated other_designation (Sasazuka Kofun) }}
'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was other_designation1 a Tochigi Prefecture other_designation.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Tochigi Prefecture-other_designation1 cultural properties|ja|栃木県指定文化財一覧}} * {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} * {{ill|Tsukayama Kofun Cluster|ja|塚山古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}} * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}

== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was other_designation1 a Tochigi Prefecture other_designation.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.

'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was other_designation1 a Tochigi Prefecture other_designation.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was other_designation1 a Tochigi Prefecture other_designation.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.
