revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sasazuka Kofun | part_of = Higashiya Kofun Cluster | image = [[File:Sasazuka Kofun.JPG|250px]] | caption = Sasazuka Kofun | location = Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|36|28|37|N|139|54|7|E}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 100 m, Height: 10.5 m | built = Mid-5th century | Buried Figure = | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}} | Designated Cultural Property = Tochigi Prefecture Designated other_designation (Sasazuka Kofun) }}
'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was other_designation1 a Tochigi Prefecture other_designation.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Tochigi Prefecture-other_designation1 cultural properties|ja|栃木県指定文化財一覧}} * {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} * {{ill|Tsukayama Kofun Cluster|ja|塚山古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}} * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}

'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was other_designation1 a Tochigi Prefecture other_designation.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Tochigi Prefecture-other_designation1 cultural properties|ja|栃木県指定文化財一覧}} * {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} * {{ill|Tsukayama Kofun Cluster|ja|塚山古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}} * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}

== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.

=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sasazuka Kofun | part_of = Higashiya Kofun Cluster | image = [[File:Sasazuka Kofun.JPG|250px]] | caption = Sasazuka Kofun | location = Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|36|28|37|N|139|54|7|E}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 100 m, Height: 10.5 m | built = Mid-5th century | Interred_individual = | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}} | Designated designation1 = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Sasazuka Kofun) }}
'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sasazuka Kofun | part_of = Higashiya Kofun Cluster | image = [[File:Sasazuka Kofun.JPG|250px]] | caption = Sasazuka Kofun | location = Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|36|28|37|N|139|54|7|E}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 100 m, Height: 10.5 m | built = Mid-5th century | Interred_individual = | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}} | Designated designation1 = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Sasazuka Kofun) }}
'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Tochigi Prefecture-Historic Site1 cultural properties|ja|栃木県指定文化財一覧}} * {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群}} * {{ill|Tsukayama Kofun Cluster|ja|塚山古墳群}} * {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}} * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}} * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sasazuka Kofun | part_of = Higashiya Kofun Cluster | image = [[File:Sasazuka Kofun.JPG|250px]] | caption = Sasazuka Kofun | location = Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|36|28|37|N|139|54|7|E}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | area = Total length: 100 m, Height: 10.5 m | built = Mid-5th century | Interred_individual = | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}}, {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=draft}} | Designated designation1 = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Sasazuka Kofun) }}
'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会|12=draft}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=draft}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=draft}}, was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=draft}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群|12=draft}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|12=draft}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=draft}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=draft}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Tochigi Prefecture-Historic Site1 cultural properties|ja|栃木県指定文化財一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsukayama Kofun Cluster|ja|塚山古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=draft}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会|12=draft}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=draft}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=draft}}, was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=draft}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群|12=draft}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|12=draft}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=draft}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=draft}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Tochigi Prefecture-Historic Site1 cultural properties|ja|栃木県指定文化財一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsukayama Kofun Cluster|ja|塚山古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=draft}}

'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=draft}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会|12=draft}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=draft}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=draft}}, was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=draft}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群|12=draft}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|12=draft}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=draft}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=draft}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|12=draft}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=draft}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=draft}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sasazuka Kofun | part_of = Higashiya Kofun Cluster | image = [[File:Sasazuka Kofun.JPG|250px]] | caption = Sasazuka Kofun | location = Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|36|28|37|N|139|54|7|E}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | area = Total length: 100 m, Height: 10.5 m | built = Mid-5th century | Interred_individual = | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}}, {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=draft}} | Designated designation1 = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Sasazuka Kofun) }}
'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会|12=draft}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=draft}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=draft}}, was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=draft}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群|12=draft}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|12=draft}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=draft}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=draft}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Tochigi Prefecture-Historic Site1 cultural properties|ja|栃木県指定文化財一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsukayama Kofun Cluster|ja|塚山古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=draft}}

burial goods recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=draft}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=draft}}, was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=draft}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群|12=draft}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|12=draft}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=draft}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=draft}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会|12=draft}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=draft}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=draft}}, was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=draft}} in southern Utsunomiya. === Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.

=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.

'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会|12=draft}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=draft}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=draft}}, was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=draft}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群|12=draft}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|12=draft}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=draft}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=draft}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Tochigi Prefecture-Historic Site1 cultural properties|ja|栃木県cultural property一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsukayama Kofun Cluster|ja|塚山古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=draft}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
{{Otheruses||長崎県壱岐市にある古墳|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称=笹塚古墳 |所属=東谷古墳群 |画像=[[画像:Sasazuka Kofun.JPG|250px]] |画像キャプション=笹塚古墳 |所在地=[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]東谷町 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|28|48.8|N|139|53|54.9|E|}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=全長100[[メートル|m]]、高さ10.5m |築造年代=5世紀中頃 |被葬者= |出土品=[[円筒埴輪]]、[[朝顔形埴輪]] |指定文化財=県指定史跡（笹塚古墳） |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=笹塚古墳|lat_deg=36|lat_min=28|lat_sec=48.8|lon_deg=139|lon_min=53|lon_sec=54.9|mark=前方後円墳-西南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=栃木県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|28|48.8|N|139|53|54.9|E}}}}}} '''笹塚古墳'''（ささづかこふん）は、[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]東谷町に存在する[[前方後円墳]]である。本項では、笹塚古墳を主墳とする'''東谷古墳群'''についても記載する。
== 概要 == 笹塚古墳は、全長100[[メートル]]、後円部の径63メートル、同高さ10.5メートル、前方部の幅48メートル、同高さ8.5メートルの栃木県内有数の大型前方後円墳である。墳の周縁部の形状から盾形の周濠が廻らされていたと推定されている。後円部墳頂は削平されて薬師堂が祀られており、薬師堂南側には盗掘の跡が残るほか、墳丘上の遺物として[[円筒埴輪]]の破片が発見された。[[2006年]]（[[平成]]18年）には宇都宮市によって[[発掘調査]]が行われ、二重周濠と三段墳丘が確認されたほか、葺石を検出した。[[遺物]]としては、[[埴輪]]が出土している。墳丘形状から推定される築成年代は[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]中期とされる。
出土品は以下のとおり。 * [[円筒埴輪]] * [[朝顔形埴輪]]
[[1957年]]（[[昭和]]32年）[[6月30日]]、同じく市内の[[戸祭大塚古墳|大塚古墳]]とともに栃木県指定史跡に指定されている<ref>{{Cite web|和書|date=|url=https://utsunomiya-8story.jp/search_post/%E7%AC%B9%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3/|title=笹塚古墳（宇都宮の歴史と文化財）|publisher=宇都宮市|accessdate=2025-03-23}}</ref>。
== 周辺環境 == 笹塚古墳は宇都宮市南部の[[田川 (利根川水系)|田川]]東岸に立地し、周辺には鶴舞塚古墳（円墳、削平され消滅）、松の塚古墳、双子塚古墳（前方部は削滅）、権現塚古墳、権現塚北古墳、車塚古墳、原古墳群などがあり、'''東谷古墳群'''を形成している。
== 東谷古墳群 == '''東谷古墳群'''は[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]東谷町にある古墳群である。最大規模の前方後円墳である笹塚古墳（上述）が主墳と考えられており、その半径500メートル以内に大小11基の古墳が確認されている。
その古墳形状から、東谷古墳群は田川西側にある[[茂原古墳群]]の後を継ぐ形で築成された、この地方の有力者の墓とみられている。
=== 鶴舞塚古墳 === 鶴舞塚古墳は、笹塚古墳の南側に位置する円墳である。周溝跡を含めると径は50メートルを超える。高さは約6メートルである。出土品は鉄[[鍬]]片、[[鉄鏃]]片などで、周溝跡の様子から笹塚古墳より後に築成されたものとみられている。
=== 双子塚古墳 === 双子塚古墳は、笹塚古墳の北側にある前方後円墳である。前方部は削平されて消失している。後円部の径は約20メートル、高さ4メートルである。埴輪は確認されていない。
=== 権現塚古墳 === 権現塚古墳は、笹塚古墳の東南東に位置する径約30メートル、高さ5メートルの円墳である。
=== 権現塚北古墳 === 権現塚北古墳は、権現塚古墳の北側にある小円墳である。
=== 松の塚古墳 === 松の塚古墳は、笹塚古墳の南西にある径約50メートルの[[帆立貝型前方後円墳]]と推定されている。その周縁部が田圃により侵食されており、この径および古墳形状は推定値である。
=== 車塚古墳群 === 車塚古墳は、笹塚古墳の東南東に位置する林の中にある径約35メートル、高さ約6メートルの円墳である。周囲の4基の円墳とともに車塚古墳群と呼ばれている。
=== 原古墳群 === 原古墳群は、笹塚古墳の東に位置する円墳2基から成る古墳群である。墳丘は、現在墓地となっている。
== アクセス == ===路線バス=== * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[宇都宮駅]]西口から ** [[関東自動車 (栃木県)|関東バス]] 上三川線に乗車約20分、「東谷」下車徒歩3分。
===車=== * [[国道121号]]（[[宇都宮環状道路|宮環]]）下横田町交差点を南進3分の[[栃木県道35号宇都宮結城線|上三川街道]]沿い東側。
==脚注== {{reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[栃木県指定文化財一覧]] * [[茂原古墳群]] * [[塚山古墳群]] * [[谷口山古墳]] * [[長岡百穴古墳]] * [[戸祭大塚古墳|大塚古墳]] * [[豊郷まほろばの道]]
{{デフォルトソート:ささつかこふん}} [[Category:栃木県の古墳]] [[Category:宇都宮市の歴史]] [[Category:栃木県指定史跡]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

| Designated designation1 = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Sasazuka Kofun)
| architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}
}}

'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Higashiya-machi, [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.

== Overview ==
Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} were discovered atop the mound.

In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya City Board of Education|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].

burial goods recovered from the site include:
* {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}
* {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}

On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.

== Surrounding Environment ==
Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.

== Higashiya Kofun Cluster ==
The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.

This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.

=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun ===
Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.

=== Futago-zuka Kofun ===
Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.

=== Gongen-zuka Kofun ===
Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.

=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun ===
Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.

=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun ===
Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.

=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster ===
Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.

=== Hara Kofun Cluster ===
The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.

== Access ==
'''By Bus'''
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR):
** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|qq=|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.

'''By Car'''
* From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|qq=|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449792}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

== Related Articles ==

* {{ill|List of Tochigi Prefecture-Historic Site1 cultural properties|ja|栃木県cultural property一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Tochigi Prefecture-Historic Site1 cultural properties|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

* {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}
* {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}

'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Higashiya-machi, [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.

== Overview ==
Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} were discovered atop the mound.

In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya City Board of Education|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].

burial goods recovered from the site include:
* {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}
* {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}

On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.

== Surrounding Environment ==
Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.

== Higashiya Kofun Cluster ==
The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.

This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.

=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun ===
Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.

=== Futago-zuka Kofun ===
Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.

=== Gongen-zuka Kofun ===
Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.

=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun ===
Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.

=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun ===
Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.

=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster ===
Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.

=== Hara Kofun Cluster ===
The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.

== Access ==
'''By Bus'''
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR):
** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|qq=|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.

'''By Car'''
* From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|qq=|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449792}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.
