'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Higashiya-machi, [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.

== Overview ==
Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} were discovered atop the mound.

In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].

burial goods recovered from the site include:
* {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}
* {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}

On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.

== Surrounding Environment ==
Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.

== Higashiya Kofun Cluster ==
The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.

This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.

=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun ===
Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.

=== Futago-zuka Kofun ===
Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.

=== Gongen-zuka Kofun ===
Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.

=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun ===
Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.

=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun ===
Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.

=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster ===
Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.

=== Hara Kofun Cluster ===
The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.

== Access ==
'''By Bus'''
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR):
** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.

'''By Car'''
* From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449792}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Higashiya-machi, [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.

== Overview ==
Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} were discovered atop the mound.

In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].

burial goods recovered from the site include:
* {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}
* {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}

On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.

== Surrounding Environment ==
Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.

== Higashiya Kofun Cluster ==
The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.

This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.

=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun ===
Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.

=== Futago-zuka Kofun ===
Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.

=== Gongen-zuka Kofun ===
Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.

=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun ===
Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.

=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun ===
Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.

=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster ===
Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.

=== Hara Kofun Cluster ===
The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.

== Access ==
'''By Bus'''
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR):
** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.

'''By Car'''
* From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449792}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Higashiya-machi, [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.

The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} were discovered atop the mound.

In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].

burial goods recovered from the site include:
* {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}
* {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}

On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.

== Surrounding Environment ==
Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.

== Higashiya Kofun Cluster ==
The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.

This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.

=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun ===
Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.

=== Futago-zuka Kofun ===
Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.

=== Gongen-zuka Kofun ===
Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.

=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun ===
Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.

=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun ===
Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.

=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster ===
Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.

=== Hara Kofun Cluster ===
The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.

== Access ==
'''By Bus'''
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR):
** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.

'''By Car'''
* From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449792}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Higashiya-machi, [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.

== Overview ==
Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} were discovered atop the mound.

In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].

burial goods recovered from the site include:
* {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}
* {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}

On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.

== Surrounding Environment ==

Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.

== Surrounding Environment ==
Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.

== Higashiya Kofun Cluster ==
The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.

This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.

=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun ===
Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.

=== Futago-zuka Kofun ===
Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.

=== Gongen-zuka Kofun ===
Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.

=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun ===
Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.

=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun ===
Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.

=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster ===
Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.

=== Hara Kofun Cluster ===
The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.

== Access ==
'''By Bus'''
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR):
** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.

'''By Car'''
* From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449792}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Higashiya-machi, [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.

== Overview ==
Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} were discovered atop the mound.

In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].

burial goods recovered from the site include:
* {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}
* {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}

On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.

== Surrounding Environment ==
Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.

This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.

=== Futago-zuka Kofun ===
Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.

=== Gongen-zuka Kofun ===
Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.

=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun ===
Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.

Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.

The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.

Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.

=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun ===
Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.

=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster ===
Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.

=== Hara Kofun Cluster ===
The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.

== Access ==
'''By Bus'''
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR):
** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.

'''By Car'''
* From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449792}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Higashiya-machi, [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.

== Overview ==
Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} were discovered atop the mound.

In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].

burial goods recovered from the site include:
* {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}
* {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}

On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.

== Surrounding Environment ==
Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.

== Higashiya Kofun Cluster ==
The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.

This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.

=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun ===
Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.

=== Futago-zuka Kofun ===
Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.

=== Gongen-zuka Kofun ===
Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.

=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun ===
Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.

=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun ===
Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.

=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster ===
Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.

=== Hara Kofun Cluster ===
The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.

== Access ==
'''By Bus'''
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR):
** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.

'''By Car'''
* From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449792}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

| Designated designation1 = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Sasazuka Kofun)
| architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}
}}

'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Higashiya-machi, [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.

== Overview ==
Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} were discovered atop the mound.

In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].

burial goods recovered from the site include:
* {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}
* {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}

On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.

== Surrounding Environment ==
Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.

== Higashiya Kofun Cluster ==
The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.

This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.

=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun ===
Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.

=== Futago-zuka Kofun ===
Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.

=== Gongen-zuka Kofun ===
Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.

=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun ===
Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.

=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun ===
Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.

=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster ===
Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.

=== Hara Kofun Cluster ===
The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.

== Access ==
'''By Bus'''
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR):
** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.

'''By Car'''
* From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449792}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Higashiya-machi, [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.

== Overview ==
Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} were discovered atop the mound.

In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].

burial goods recovered from the site include:
* {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}
* {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}

On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.

== Surrounding Environment ==
Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.

== Higashiya Kofun Cluster ==
The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.

This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.

=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun ===
Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.

=== Futago-zuka Kofun ===
Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.

=== Gongen-zuka Kofun ===
Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.

=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun ===
Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.

=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun ===
Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.

=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster ===
Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.

=== Hara Kofun Cluster ===
The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.

== Access ==
'''By Bus'''
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR):
** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.

'''By Car'''
* From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449792}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Higashiya-machi, [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]], Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.

== Overview ==
Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} were discovered atop the mound.

In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].

burial goods recovered from the site include:
* {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}
* {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}

On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.

== Surrounding Environment ==
Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.

== Higashiya Kofun Cluster ==
The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.

This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.

=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun ===
Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.

=== Futago-zuka Kofun ===
Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.

=== Gongen-zuka Kofun ===
Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.

=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun ===
Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.

=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun ===
Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.

=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster ===
Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.

=== Hara Kofun Cluster ===
The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.

== Access ==
'''By Bus'''
* From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|Utsunomiya Station]] (JR):
** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.

'''By Car'''
* From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449792}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

| architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}
}}

'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.

== Overview ==
Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} were discovered atop the mound.

In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi}} were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}.

burial goods recovered from the site include:
* {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}
* {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}

On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.

== Surrounding Environment ==
Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.

== Higashiya Kofun Cluster ==
The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.

This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.

=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun ===
Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.

=== Futago-zuka Kofun ===
Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.

=== Gongen-zuka Kofun ===
Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.

=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun ===
Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.

=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun ===
Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.

=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster ===
Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.

=== Hara Kofun Cluster ===
The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.

== Access ==
'''By Bus'''
* From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|en|Utsunomiya Station|WD=Q801549|lt=Utsunomiya Station}} (JR):
** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.

'''By Car'''
* From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449792}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi}} were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|en|Utsunomiya Station|WD=Q801549|lt=Utsunomiya Station}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449792}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of Tochigi Prefecture-Historic Site1 cultural properties|ja|栃木県cultural property一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Tochigi Prefecture-Historic Site1 cultural properties|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tsukayama Kofun Cluster|ja|塚山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukayama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchi-yama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}} * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

{{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sasazuka Kofun | part_of = Higashiya Kofun Cluster | image = [[File:Sasazuka Kofun.JPG|250px]] | caption = Sasazuka Kofun | location = {{ill|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.476944444444|N|139.90194444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 100 m, Height: 10.5 m | built = Mid-5th century | Interred_individual = | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}} | Designated designation1 = Tochigi Prefecture Designated Historic Site (Sasazuka Kofun) | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }}
'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi}} were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} in southern Utsunomiya. == Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|en|Utsunomiya Station|WD=Q801549|lt=Utsunomiya Station}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Kantō Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449792}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya|1=Utsunomiya}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.
== Overview == Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} were discovered atop the mound.
In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}.
burial goods recovered from the site include: * {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} * {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}
On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.
== Surrounding Environment == Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.
== Higashiya Kofun Cluster == The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.
This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.
=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun === Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.
=== Futago-zuka Kofun === Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.
=== Gongen-zuka Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.
=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun === Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.
=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun === Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.
=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster === Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.
=== Hara Kofun Cluster === The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.
== Access == '''By Bus''' * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|en|Utsunomiya Station|WD=Q801549|lt=Utsunomiya Station|1=Utsunomiya Station}} (JR): ** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Q11655984|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.
'''By Car''' * From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449792}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.

'''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya|1=Utsunomiya}}, Tochigi Prefecture. This article also covers the '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''', with Sasazuka Kofun as its primary mound.

== Overview ==
Sasazuka Kofun is one of the largest keyhole-shaped kofun in Tochigi Prefecture, measuring 100 meters in total length. The rear circular section has a diameter of 63 meters and a height of 10.5 meters, while the front rectangular section measures 48 meters wide and 8.5 meters high. A shield-shaped moat likely surrounded the mound, based on its peripheral layout. The summit of the rear circular section has been flattened and now hosts a Yakushi-dō (hall dedicated to Yakushi Buddha). Evidence of tomb robbery is visible on the southern side of the Yakushi-dō, and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} were discovered atop the mound.

In 2006 (Heisei 18), the {{ill|Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja|宇都宮市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya City Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conducted an excavation, confirming a double moat and a three-tiered mound structure. Traces of {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} were also identified. Based on the mound's type, it is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-5th century, during the middle {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}.

burial goods recovered from the site include:
* {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}
* {{ill|Morning glory-shaped haniwa|ja|朝顔形埴輪|1=Morning glory-shaped haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Morning glory-shaped haniwa|qq=}}

On June 30, 1956 (Showa 31), Sasazuka Kofun, along with {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, was Historic Site1 a Tochigi Prefecture Historic Site.

== Surrounding Environment ==
Sasazuka Kofun is situated on the eastern bank of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River (Tone River System)|qq=}} in southern Utsunomiya. Nearby are several other Kofuns, including Tsurumai-zuka Kofun (a destroyed round kofun), Matsuno-zuka Kofun, Futago-zuka Kofun (with its front section destroyed), Gongen-zuka Kofun, Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun, Kuruma-zuka Kofun, and the Hara Kofun Cluster, collectively typeing the Higashiya Kofun Cluster.

== Higashiya Kofun Cluster ==
The '''Higashiya Kofun Cluster''' is located in Higashiya-machi, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture. Sasazuka Kofun is considered the primary mound, with 11 smaller mounds located within a 500-meter radius.

This cluster is believed to represent the graves of regional elites from the area, succeeding the {{ill|Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja|茂原古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mobara Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the western bank of the Tagawa River.

=== Tsurumai-zuka Kofun ===
Tsurumai-zuka Kofun is a round mound located south of Sasazuka Kofun. Its diameter exceeds 50 meters, with a height of approximately 6 meters. burial goods include fragments of iron hoes and iron arrowheads. The moat remains suggest it was constructed after Sasazuka Kofun.

=== Futago-zuka Kofun ===
Futago-zuka Kofun is a keyhole-shaped kofun north of Sasazuka Kofun. Its front section has been destroyed, leaving only the rear circular section with a diameter of about 20 meters and a height of 4 meters. No haniwa have been found.

=== Gongen-zuka Kofun ===
Gongen-zuka Kofun is a round mound southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, measuring 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height.

=== Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun ===
Gongen-zuka Kita Kofun is a small round mound located north of Gongen-zuka Kofun.

=== Matsuno-zuka Kofun ===
Matsuno-zuka Kofun is a scallop-shaped keyhole mound southwest of Sasazuka Kofun. The mound's periphery has been eroded by rice fields, and its estimated diameter and type are approximations.

=== Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster ===
Kuruma-zuka Kofun is a round mound located southeast of Sasazuka Kofun, within a wooded area. It has a diameter of about 35 meters and a height of approximately 6 meters. Together with four nearby round mounds, it types the Kuruma-zuka Kofun Cluster.

=== Hara Kofun Cluster ===
The Hara Kofun Cluster is located east of Sasazuka Kofun and consists of two round mounds, which are currently used as graveyards.

== Access ==
'''By Bus'''
* From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|en|Utsunomiya Station|WD=Q801549|lt=Utsunomiya Station|1=Utsunomiya Station}} (JR):
** Take the {{ill|Kantō Bus|ja|関東自動車|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus|1=Q11655984|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655984}} Kamimikawa Line for approximately 20 minutes. Get off at "Higashiya" and walk 3 minutes.

'''By Car'''
* From the National Route 121 ({{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|1=Utsunomiya Ring Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449792}}) Shitayokota Intersection, drive south for 3 minutes along Kamimikawa Highway (Prefectural Route 35). The site is located on the east side of the road.
